title: Tool results are plain text, not structured JSON content
evidence: src/br_soccer_mcp_handler.erl:617 text_content/1 wraps every result as a single {type:text} block; standings/stats are human-formatted strings
suggestion: Optionally return structured JSON alongside text so MCP clients can consume results programmatically

title: OTP application/supervisor scaffolding is unused by the stdio escript path
evidence: src/br_soccer_mcp_sup.erl:9 init/1 returns empty ChildSpecs; br_soccer_mcp_main:main/1 calls br_soccer_mcp_server:start/1 directly, bypassing the supervisor
suggestion: Either run the server under the supervisor or drop the app/sup modules to reduce dead scaffolding

title: competition_standings does not normalize team names, so state-variant clubs split
evidence: src/br_soccer_query.erl:108 compute_standings uses raw home/away team names by design (comment line 107); Atletico-MG and Atletico-PR stay separate, but inconsistent suffix formats across datasets could fragment one club
suggestion: Document the intended granularity or normalize when a single competition uses consistent naming

title: Date-range filtering is season/year only
evidence: src/br_soccer_query.erl:24 filter_by_season matches a season string or 4-char year prefix; no explicit from/to date-range filter is exposed as a tool argument
suggestion: R4 (season filter) is satisfied; a true date-range filter would extend it
